data.title: A whale accurately shorted BTC, with unrealized profits exceeding $7.5 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale has performed excellent shorting operations in the recent BTC price fluctuations, with a cumulative unrealized profit of more than $7.5 million. The investor shorted BTC at about $96,500 on February 22. Within a week, BTC fell below $80,000, and then perfectly closed the position at a low of about $78,900. After the release of Trump's executive order, BTC soared to more than $94,000, and he accurately re-shorted at a high of about $94,424. At present, the investor has set up a new short position in the range of $92,449-92,636, and set a profit-taking order in the range of $70,475-74,192.

data.title: Elon Musk's GSAi Chatbot Deployed for Government Automation
data.description:
According to PANews, Elon Musk's company DOGE has launched its custom chatbot, GSAi, within the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) to automate government tasks. The tool assists employees in drafting emails, preparing speech points, summarizing texts, and coding, as well as analyzing contracts and procurement data.

GSAi offers three AI model options: Claude Haiku 3.5 (default), Claude Sonnet 3.5 v2, and Meta Llama 3.2. Currently, the tool is available to approximately 1,500 employees, with plans for broader implementation in the future. Users are instructed not to input federal non-public information, personal identity information, or controlled unclassified information.

data.title: Ethereum Faces Potential On-Chain Liquidation Of $128 Million
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from DefiLlama indicates that Ethereum is facing a potential on-chain liquidation amounting to $128 million at the price point of $1,919.266. The majority of this liquidation is associated with the MakerDAO protocol, accounting for $127.8 million.

data.title: Apex Fintech Considers Acquiring Bakkt Amid Rising Market Interest
data.description: According to Odaily, Apex Fintech, a fintech company specializing in integrating stock trading into applications, is contemplating the acquisition of Bakkt, a cryptocurrency platform initially spun off by Intercontinental Exchange (ICE). This potential acquisition comes two years after Bakkt acquired Apex's crypto business for up to $200 million, a deal seemingly influenced by strict regulations under the Biden administration. Following the report by Axios, Bakkt's stock price surged approximately 15% in early trading, reaching $14.86, drawing significant market attention.

data.title: South Korea Considers Bitcoin For National Reserve And Stablecoin Development
data.description: According to Foresight News, South Korean financial experts and opposition party members have proposed incorporating Bitcoin into the national reserve and developing a stablecoin backed by the Korean won. The seminar aimed to analyze potential strategies in response to the United States' Bitcoin-centric national reserve initiatives. Kim Jong-seung, CEO of blockchain company xCrypton, emphasized the need for South Korea to respond with clear policies during an event preceding U.S. President Donald Trump's executive order announcement.

data.title: Mark Carney Elected As New Leader Of Canada's Liberal Party
data.description: According to Foresight News, Canada's ruling Liberal Party announced yesterday that Mark Carney has been elected as the new leader of the party. Carney is set to succeed the current Prime Minister, Justin Trudeau, and will form a new cabinet. Previously, in 2021, Carney had shared a tweet by Elon Musk regarding Bitcoin payments, expressing his support for a resilient, high-performance, and carbon-free payment system.

data.title: A whale deposited 1.14 million USDC into Hyperliquid and went long on Bitcoin with 20x leverage
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, as Bitcoin fell, some whales began to go long on Bitcoin.
One of the addresses deposited 1.14 million USDC to Hyperliquid and went long on Bitcoin with 20x leverage.
Another whale spent $4 million to set up 100 limit orders to go long on BTC, with a price range of $76,000 to $79,000.

data.title: Coingecko plans to launch an upgrade on March 24-25, and platform data may be temporarily affected
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Crypto data platform Coingecko announced that it will carry out maintenance and upgrade of network infrastructure from 1:00 am on Monday, March 24 to 9:00 am on Tuesday, March 25 UTC. Although the service should continue to operate, information such as cryptocurrency prices and market data on the platform may be affected, and there may also be some downtime.

data.title: South Korean opposition and industry representatives call for Bitcoin to be included in national reserves
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News South Korean financial experts and opposition politicians recently proposed at a congressional forum to include Bitcoin in the national reserve and promote the development of stablecoins backed by the Korean won. This discussion comes at a time when the United States is advancing its Bitcoin reserve plan.
According to the Korea Herald, the seminar was hosted by the largest opposition party, the Democratic Party, and focused on analyzing the impact of the United States' promotion of Bitcoin reserves. Just the day after the forum, US President Trump signed an executive order to formally establish Bitcoin reserves and crypto asset reserves.
Earlier news, the White House held its first cryptocurrency summit on Friday afternoon, and industry leaders such as MicroStrategy Chairman Michael Saylor and Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong were invited to participate. Trump emphasized at the summit that he hopes to promote a "cryptocurrency revival." (Decrypt)

data.title: Trump family project WLFI portfolio lost $110 million, with ETH suffering the most losses
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Yu Jin's analysis, WLFI's current total portfolio loss has reached $110 million, and its 9 cryptocurrencies purchased with $336 million in funds now only have $226 million left.
Among them, ETH accounts for 65%, which is also the asset with the largest loss. The average cost price of ETH is 3,240, while the current price is only 2,000, with a loss of up to $80.85 million (-37%). In contrast, the smallest decline is TRX, which has only fallen by 5% since WLFI bought it.
